title: [Cofagrigus] Battle Guide
summary: A 145 Def [Ghost] [wall] that flips the [mummy] ability onto attackers to delete [huge-power], [guts], and [moxie] on contact.
strengths: 145 Def [bold] [wall]s nearly every physical attacker in the format
[mummy] overwrites enemy abilities on contact — a hard [counter] to [guts] and [huge-power]
[will-o-wisp] and [pain-split] create a unique sustain loop
weaknesses: 30 Speed means every move lands on it first
105 SpD isn't enough to live two hits from top special attackers

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Cofagrigus] is built a[round] one stat and one ability — base 145 Def and [mummy]. The ability replaces an attacker's ability with [mummy] the moment they make contact, which cleanly [disable]s some of the most dangerous offensive abilities in the format ([huge-power], [guts], [moxie], protean). That [sing]le-c[lick] interaction matters more than most of [Cofagrigus]'s moves.

## Strengths
With a [bold] nature and full defensive investment, [Cofagrigus] [wall]s the entire physical tier. Mummy turns any attacker that c[lick]s a contact move into a neutered shell — Diggersby goes from [huge-power] to [mummy], Conkeldurr loses [iron-fist], Krookodile loses [moxie]. [will-o-wisp] [cut]s physical damage by half on top of that, so most physical sweepers effectively waste a turn c[lick]ing into [Cofagrigus]. [pain-split] is unique as a [wall] tool — with only 58 HP base, [Cofagrigus] almost always gains HP from this while also draining the oppo[sing] attacker.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ghost] and [Dark] are 2x weaknesses and both are common — a [Dark]-type pivot's [knock-off] or [dark]-pulse forces [Cofagrigus] out immediately. 105 SpD holds up okay but isn't enough to stand in front of a top-tier special attacker. Speed 30 means every move lands first, so a [taunt] before [will-o-wisp] shuts the whole plan down, which is why [mental-herb] matters. [pain-split] also backfires when the opponent has less HP than [Cofagrigus], so it isn't a blind [recover]y c[lick].

## Recommended Moveset
[will-o-wisp] is the locked utility — half of [Cofagrigus]'s role is burning physical attackers on entry. [shadow-ball] is the only real attack; off 95 SpA it's not huge but it keeps [psychic] and [Ghost] attackers from sitting for free. [pain-split] [substitute]s for [recover] and also acts as a second form of chip on the opponent. [haze] is the fourth slot to erase setup sweeper boosts — a [sing]le turn of [haze] saves the game against [dragon-dance]. [hex] is a viable alternative to [shadow-ball] when the build runs [will-o-wisp] every game, doubling damage on burned targets.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default — [Cofagrigus] needs every sustain source it can stack. [sitrus-berry] is the alternative for builds that want a [sing]le big heal on entry rather than chip [recover]y. [mental-herb] is a sideways pick into teams that lead with [taunt]. The ability is locked as [mummy] and that's precisely the point of the Pokémon. Tera [Fairy] is the strongest defensive Tera, flipping both the [Ghost] and [Dark] weaknesses into resistances and immunities while gaining a [Fighting] resistance. Tera [Dark] is a more aggressive pivot, removing the [psychic] threat space entirely.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Cofagrigus] covers the physical half of the defensive core — pair it with a dedicated special [wall] (Blissey, Chansey, or a [calm-mind] [Fairy]). A [Dark]-resist partner ([Fighting] or [Fairy]) answers the matchup where [Cofagrigus] cannot stay in. A hazard setter amplifies [pain-split] damage by reducing opponent HP on every switch. [Cofagrigus] fits naturally into a [trick-room] team thanks to its base 30 Speed, where it can c[lick] moves first for a change.

## Play Patterns
Bring [Cofagrigus] in specifically on physical setup sweepers — the turn a [swords-dance] or [dragon-dance] is telegraphed is the right entry. C[lick] [will-o-wisp] first, then [haze] if a boost landed, then [shadow-ball] or [pain-split] based on opponent HP. Never stay in against [dark]-pulse or [shadow-ball] attackers. Save Tera for the matchup where [Cofagrigus] is the only answer to a [Dark] or [Ghost] breaker, because lo[sing] it early usually means lo[sing] the game state entirely.
